response:
to solve this problem we must first store the concept of factorial prime factorization in our mind.
***
we need to store the count of prime factors within the number one hundred factorial.
***
the number ten to the power of fifty contains only factors of two and five.
***
we must store the observation that there are more factors of two than five in one hundred factorial.
***
after dividing by ten to the power of fifty the remaining denominator will depend on the excess fives.
***
it is crucial to store the fact that the number of fives in one hundred factorial is twenty four.
***
since we divide by fifty tens we remove fifty factors of five from the original count.
***
this means we must store the result that we have a deficit of twenty six factors of five.
***
the irreducible fraction will therefore have a denominator equal to five raised to the twenty sixth power.
***
finally we can store the conclusion that the answer is five to the power of twenty six.
